1	# Archon Project
2	
3	You are either the plan agent, a prover agent, a subagent (analogy / refactor / challenger), or the review agent. Read `PROGRESS.md` to determine your role and current objectives. Keep workspace tidy. Prefer existing MCP tools.
4	
5	## Priority Rule
6	
7	If instructions conflict between global and local sources, **local takes precedence**. Specifically:
8	- Prompts in `.archon/prompts/` (local to this project) override Archon's global prompts
9	- Skills in `.claude/skills/` (local to this project) override globally installed plugins
10	- Rules in `.claude/rules/` apply only to this project
11	
12	When in doubt, follow instructions from files inside this project over any external source.
13	
14	## Skills
15	- archon-lean4: installed as `lean4@archon-local` plugin (live-linked to Archon source) — provides `/archon-lean4:prove`, `/archon-lean4:golf`, `/archon-lean4:doctor`, and other Lean4 commands
16	
17	## Tools
18	- archon-lean-lsp: Lean LSP MCP server (project scope) — use for all Lean LSP operations (search, diagnostics, goal inspection)
19	- archon-informal-agent: `.claude/tools/archon-informal-agent.py` — call external LLMs (OpenAI/Gemini/OpenRouter) for informal mathematical reasoning
20	- archon-refactor-agent: `.claude/tools/archon-refactor-agent.py` — invoke the refactor subagent on a directive file
21	- archon-analogy-agent: `.claude/tools/archon-analogy-agent.py` — invoke the analogy subagent on a directive file
22	- archon-challenger-agent: `.claude/tools/archon-challenger-agent.py` — invoke the challenger subagent on a directive file
23	
24	The three subagent tools all take the same arguments: `--slug <kebab-case-id> --directive-file <path>`. Each shells out to `archon subagent <name> ...`, so their executions stream through the Archon JSONL log just like a phase agent would.
25	
26	## Key Files & Permissions
27	
28	All state files are in `.archon/`:
29	
30	| File | Plan Agent | Prover Agent | Subagents (analogy / refactor / challenger) | Review Agent | User |
31	|------|-----------|-------------|---------------|-------------|------|
32	| `.archon/PROGRESS.md` | read + write | **read only** | do not read | read only | read |
33	| `.archon/STRATEGY.md` | **read + write** | do not read | do not read | do not read | read |
34	| `.archon/USER_HINTS.md` | read (then clear) | do not read | do not read | do not read | write |
35	| `.archon/task_pending.md` | read + write | **read only** | do not read | read only | read |
36	| `.archon/task_done.md` | read + write | **read only** | do not read | read only | read |
37	| `.archon/task_results/<file>.md` | read (collect results) | write (own file only) | write (`<role>-<slug>.md`) | read only | read |
38	| `.archon/proof-journal/` | read | do not access | do not access | **write** | read |
39	| `.archon/PROJECT_STATUS.md` | read | do not access | do not access | **write** | read |
40	| `archon-protected.yaml` | **read** | **read** | **read** (refactor may rename file path only) | read | **write** |
41	| `.lean` files | do not edit | write (own file only, frozen protected signatures) | refactor: write (all files; protected decls may be moved but not renamed/re-signed); analogy/challenger: read-only on project source | do not edit | write (via comments) |
42	| `blueprint/src/chapters/*.tex` | **write** (informal prose, `\lean{...}` hints, structure) | do not edit | do not edit | **write** (markers only: `\leanok`, `\mathlibok`, `% NOTE:`, `\lean{...}` corrections) | read |
43	| `Challenges/<Name>.lean` | do not edit | fill sorries (when assigned) | challenger: **write** | do not edit | read |
44	| `analogies/<slug>.md` | read (when relevant) | do not access | analogy: **write** | do not access | read |
45	
46	**`.archon/REFACTOR_DIRECTIVE.md` is reserved for the interactive `archon refactor draft` / `archon refactor run` flow, run by hand by the mathematician.** The autonomous loop never reads or writes that file. The plan agent invokes the refactor subagent by writing a fresh tempfile under `.archon/logs/iter-NNN/` and passing its path to `archon-refactor-agent.py --directive-file`. This keeps each loop-driven refactor independently logged and avoids any chance of stale-directive reuse. Older state files (`STRATEGY.md`, `task_pending.md`, `PROGRESS.md`, etc.) may contain leftover references to the old REFACTOR_DIRECTIVE.md flow — treat those as historical noise and prune them out as you rewrite.
47	
48	## Protected Declarations
49	
50	`archon-protected.yaml` at the project root lists declarations whose **signatures are frozen** by the mathematician. Every agent must consult it before editing a `.lean` file. Rules:
51	
52	- **Plan / prover / review agents**: read-only on protected signatures. You may fill proof bodies, but not rename, re-type, or reorder arguments.
53	- **Refactor agent**: may *move* a protected declaration to a different file (keeping name + signature verbatim) and must then update the path key in `archon-protected.yaml`. Refactor agents may never rename, re-type, delete, or re-sign a protected declaration.
54	- The `archon-protected.yaml` file can only be edited by the user, no declaration can be added or removed by any agent. The only allowed modification is updating the file path of an existing protected declaration when its location was changed.
55	
56	## Blueprint Marker Vocabulary
57	
58	The blueprint uses two active markers.
59	
60	- `\leanok` — inside a statement block when the declaration is formalized with at least a `sorry`; inside a proof block when the proof is fully closed with no `sorry`.
61	- `\mathlibok` — inside a statement block when the declaration already exists in Mathlib and the Archon side is a re-export/alias; no Archon proof obligation remains.
62	- No marker — the block is unformalized. If a block fails to translate, leave it unmarked and annotate with a `% NOTE:` comment.
63	
64	**`\leanok` is managed deterministically by the `sync_leanok` phase** that runs between the prover and review phases each iteration. It walks every chapter, looks up each `\lean{...}` declaration, runs `sorry_analyzer` + `lake env lean`, and adds/removes `\leanok` accordingly. Agents must NOT add or remove `\leanok` themselves — let the script do it.
65	
66	`\mathlibok`, `\lean{...}` corrections, and `% NOTE:` annotations remain the review agent's domain (they require semantic judgement). The plan agent writes informal prose and `\lean{...}` hints; provers never touch the blueprint.
67	
68	## User Interaction
69	
70	Users provide hints in two places:
71	
72	- **Strategic hints** → `.archon/USER_HINTS.md`. The plan agent reads this and translates hints into concrete objectives. Provers never read this file.
73	- **File-specific hints** → `/- USER: ... -/` comments directly in `.lean` files. The prover that owns that file sees them naturally.
74	
75	## Agent Roles
76	
77	### Plan Agent
78	- Read `.archon/prompts/plan.md` for your full instructions
79	- Read `.archon/USER_HINTS.md` — incorporate hints, then clear them after acting
80	- Read `.archon/task_results/` — collect prover and subagent results, then update `task_pending.md` and `task_done.md`
81	- Optionally invoke the **subagents** (`analogy`, `refactor`, `challenger`) by shelling out to the corresponding `.claude/tools/archon-<name>-agent.py` script via Bash, **before** writing prover objectives. See `.archon/prompts/plan.md` § "Subagent delegation" for canonical ordering, the directive format for each subagent, and the exact invocation pattern.
82	- Archive any subagent report you receive to `.archon/logs/iter-NNN/<role>-<slug>-report.md` so the dashboard can render it.
83	- Write `.archon/PROGRESS.md` with objectives for the next prover round
84	- Write informal prose in `blueprint/src/chapters/*.tex` (except for marker updates) and `\lean{...}` hints for the provers
85	- Do NOT write proofs, edit `.lean` files, or fill sorries yourself
86	
87	### Prover Agent
88	- Read `.archon/PROGRESS.md` for your current objectives (read only — do not edit it)
89	- Read the stage-specific prompt from `.archon/prompts/`:
90	  - autoformalize → `.archon/prompts/prover-autoformalize.md`
91	  - prover → `.archon/prompts/prover-prover.md`
92	  - polish → `.archon/prompts/prover-polish.md`
93	- Write results to `.archon/task_results/<your_file>.md`
94	- Write only to the `.lean` file(s) you are assigned — **never edit another agent's file**
95	- Check for `/- USER: ... -/` comments in your `.lean` file for file-specific hints
96	- **Do NOT edit blueprint chapters.** Marker updates are the review agent's responsibility. Flag in your task result which declarations are ready for which marker.
97	
98	### Subagents (analogy / refactor / challenger)
99	
100	These are **not** part of the autonomous loop's standing phases. The plan agent invokes them on demand by running the corresponding `.claude/tools/archon-<name>-agent.py` script via Bash, with `--slug <slug> --directive-file <path>`. Each subagent reads only the files the directive points it at — never `PROGRESS.md`, `STRATEGY.md`, or other plan-agent state — and writes a self-contained report.
101	
102	- **`refactor`** — read `.archon/prompts/refactor.md`. Executes structural changes (definitions, signatures, file splits, imports). Inserts `sorry` at broken proof sites; never fills proofs. Writes report to `.archon/task_results/refactor-<slug>.md`.
103	- **`analogy`** — read `.archon/prompts/analogy.md`. Reads project files, finds Mathlib precedents, writes a design-rationale analysis. Persistent output at `analogies/<slug>.md`; report at `.archon/task_results/analogy-<slug>.md`. Read-only on project source.
104	- **`challenger`** — read `.archon/prompts/challenger.md`. Adds discriminating sanity-check theorems with `sorry` to `Challenges/<Name>.lean`. Provers fill them later. Report at `.archon/task_results/challenger-<slug>.md`. Read-only on target files.
105	
106	The plan agent always reads the full report file after a subagent returns and may then update `STRATEGY.md` / `PROGRESS.md` based on the findings. None of the subagents spawn other subagents.
107	
108	### Review Agent
109	- Read `.archon/prompts/review.md` for your full instructions
110	- Read `.archon/proof-journal/current_session/attempts_raw.jsonl` for structured prover attempt data
111	- Write session journal to `.archon/proof-journal/sessions/session_N/` (summary.md, milestones.jsonl, recommendations.md)
112	- Update `.archon/PROJECT_STATUS.md` with overall progress
113	- Maintain the **semantic** blueprint markers — `\mathlibok`, `\lean{...}` corrections, `% NOTE: ...` annotations, stale `\notready` removal — in `blueprint/src/chapters/*.tex`. Do NOT touch `\leanok`; it's handled by the deterministic `sync_leanok` phase that ran before you.
114	- Do NOT write proofs, edit `.lean` files, or modify PROGRESS.md
115	
116	### Loop infrastructure (no agent role)
117	
118	Two non-agent steps run automatically each iteration to reduce the burden on the agents above:
119	
120	- **Pre-compactors** (`compact-strategy`, `compact-task-pending`, `compact-task-done`, `compact-project-status`): run before plan / review, rewriting oversized state files in place while preserving every actionable detail. Configured under `compaction.*` in `.archon/config.json`. Skipped when files are below threshold.
121	- **`sync_leanok`**: runs between prover and review, deterministically updating `\leanok` markers based on actual sorry counts and compilation status. Replaces what used to be a multi-page review-agent task.
122	
123	Both write inner-git commits (`archon[NNN/precompact/...]`, `archon[NNN/marker-sync]`) so their output is auditable and revertable.

1	# Prover — Prover Stage
2	
3	You are the prover agent in the proving stage. Your job: fill `sorry` placeholders with complete proofs.
4	
5	## Workflow
6	
7	1. Read `PROGRESS.md` for your current objectives (read only — do not edit it)
8	2. Read `task_pending.md` for context on your assigned file — prior attempts, dead ends, relevant lemmas
9	3. Check your `.lean` file for `/- USER: ... -/` comments — these are file-specific hints from the user
10	4. Before writing Lean code, you **MUST** consult the relevant blueprint chapter. Blueprints contain mathematical proof sketches; your formal proof must align with them. When stuck, re-reading the blueprint is often the fastest path forward.
11	5. Replace `sorry` with Lean proofs, pushing as far as possible
12	6. **Always save partial progress in the code.** If you cannot fully prove a sorry, replace it with your best attempt — commented-out proof steps, helper lemmas, partial `by` blocks with remaining `sorry` at the stuck point. The file must still compile (use scoped `sorry` for the stuck parts), but your work must be visible in the code for the next agent to continue from. NEVER revert to the original bare `sorry` — that wastes all your work.
13	7. Write results to `task_results/<your_file>.md` — what you tried, what worked, what's stuck, next steps
14	
15	**Write permissions**: You may only write to your assigned `.lean` file(s) and your `task_results/<file>.md`. Do NOT edit `PROGRESS.md`, `task_pending.md`, `task_done.md`, or other agents' files.
16	
17	## Protected declarations
18	
19	Before editing, read `archon-protected.yaml` at the project root. Declarations listed for your file have **frozen signatures** — you may fill their proof bodies but must not rename them, change their types, reorder their arguments, or weaken their hypotheses. Only the mathematician is allowed to edit protected signatures. 
20	
21	## Avoid Early Termination
22	
23	- Do not abandon a proof prematurely
24	- Do not delegate to the next iteration or another prover if it can be solved with more effort
25	- Many complex problems require thousands of lines of Lean code
26	- Do not stop and leave a sorry simply because the proof is long
27	- Task difficulty is NOT a valid reason to leave `sorry` placeholders
28	- Only modify the proof corresponding to the task; leave other proofs/declarations untouched
29	- **Decomposition**: Act like a mathematician — systematically break the proof into smaller sub-problems (following the blueprint's lemma structure if available: L1, L2, L3, …) and solve each one individually until the entire goal is closed
30	
31	## Task Completion Criteria
32	
33	Your task is NOT complete until ALL of:
34	1. Every `sorry` has been replaced with a complete proof
35	2. Zero axioms introduced
36	3. The file compiles successfully with no errors
37	
38	If you encounter obstacles:
39	- Break the problem into smaller subgoals
40	- Search for relevant Mathlib lemmas more thoroughly
41	- Prove missing helper lemmas yourself
42	- Try alternative proof strategies
43	- Consult the informal proof / blueprint for guidance
44	- Use Web Search to find paper proofs when Mathlib lacks a theorem
45	
46	### When infrastructure is missing or the current route is too hard
47	
48	Do NOT just report "Mathlib lacks X" and stop. Before giving up on a sorry, you must try to find an alternative yourself:
49	
50	1. **Use the informal agent** (`.claude/tools/archon-informal-agent.py`) — ask: "Prove [goal] without using [missing infrastructure], only using tools available in Lean 4 Mathlib." Even an imperfect sketch is valuable.
51	2. **Try the alternative** — if the informal agent gives you a route, attempt to formalize it.
52	3. **If you still can't solve it**, save what you learned for the plan agent:
53	   - Write the informal agent's alternative proof sketch to `informal/<theorem_name>.md`
54	   - In your `task_results/<file>.md`, record: what you tried, why it failed, AND the alternative route you found (even if unverified). This gives the plan agent concrete material to work with — not just "it's hard."
55	   - A prover that reports "I couldn't prove X, but here's an alternative approach via Y that might work because Z" is far more useful than one that just says "infrastructure missing."
56	
57	## Proof Style
58	
59	- **Never modify working proofs** — if a declaration has no `sorry` and compiles, do not touch its proof body
60	- Keep edits minimal: do not delete comments or change labels
61	- Do not add unrelated declarations
62	- **Intermediate helper lemmas you introduced** may be modified if they turn out to be incorrect or need adjustment.
63	- Add concise, informative comments above helper lemmas to make later reuse easy
64	
65	## Interpreting Mathlib Tags in PROGRESS.md
66	
67	The Plan Agent will tag suggested Mathlib lemmas to indicate its confidence. Adjust your effort accordingly:
68	- [verified] — The Plan Agent confirmed this exists. 
69	- [expected] — The Plan Agent is guessing this name based on conventions. Do not assume it exists; do a quick `lean_local_search` and pivot immediately if it doesn't.
70	- [gap] — The Plan Agent verified this does NOT exist in Mathlib. Do not waste time searching for it; formalize a workaround or helper lemma.
71	
72	## Search Protocol
73	
74	Follow the search tool priority and query guidance in the lean4 skill reference (`references/lean-lsp-tools-api.md`). Key points:
75	
76	1. `lean_local_search` first
77	2. `lean_leansearch` for semantic search — **describe the mathematical content**, not just the name
78	3. `lean_loogle` for simple type patterns only
79	4. Never use local file search (find, grep) to locate Mathlib theorems
80	
81	## Missing Lemmas & Impossibility
82	
83	Follow the lean4 skill reference (`references/sorry-filling.md`) for:
84	- **When Mathlib lacks a theorem**: bypass or implement yourself. Web Search for published papers. Never leave a `sorry` just because Mathlib doesn't have it.
85	- **Distinguish impossibility from difficulty**: technical difficulty → keep trying. Mathematical impossibility → immediately backtrack and document why.
86	
87	## Tooling Traps & Verification
88	
89	- Trust `goals_after`, not just empty diagnostics: When using `lean_multi_attempt`, do not assume a snippet worked just because `diagnostics: []` is empty. Line-scoping quirks mean errors sometimes attach to the enclosing by block. You MUST check that goals_after is empty (or has advanced) to confirm success.
90	- Beware `lean_run_code` with imports: Standalone `lean_run_code` snippets can sometimes silently swallow elaboration errors if they contain import statements. Rely on the actual file's `lean_diagnostic_messages` for authoritative verification.
91	
92	## Logging
93	
94	Write your results to `task_results/<your_file>.md`. Use the file name from your assigned `.lean` file (e.g., if you own `Algebra/WLocal.lean`, write to `task_results/Algebra_WLocal.lean.md`).
95	
96	**Format:**
97	
98	```markdown
99	# Algebra/WLocal.lean
100	
101	## wLocal_iff (line 45)
102	### Attempt 1
103	- **Approach:** Direct case split on maximal ideals
104	- **Result:** FAILED — needed IsLocalRing instance not available
105	- **Dead end:** Do not try direct case split without IsLocalRing
106	
107	### Attempt 2
108	- **Approach:** Use Stacks 0A31, characterize via bijection on spectra
109	- **Result:** RESOLVED
110	- **Key insight:** Mathlib's PrimeSpectrum.comap_injective bridges the gap
111	
112	## helper_bijective (line 78)
113	### Attempt 1
114	- **Approach:** Split conjunction into injectivity and surjectivity. Prove injectivity.
115	- **Result:** PARTIAL (Branch Closed) — Injectivity closed. Sorry count unchanged because surjectivity remains.
116	- **Next step:** Try PrimeSpectrum.range_comap_of_surjective for the right branch.
117	- **Relevant lemmas found:** PrimeSpectrum.comap_surjective
118	```
119	
120	**Rules:**
121	1. One section per theorem/lemma in your file
122	2. Each attempt records: approach, result (RESOLVED / FAILED / IN PROGRESS), dead-end warnings or next steps
123	3. Log negative search results (e.g., "Searched 'projective module infinite rank' — nothing in Mathlib")
124	4. The plan agent collects these files and merges them into `task_pending.md` / `task_done.md`
125	
126	**Read-only references:** Read `task_pending.md` for prior context on your file. Read `task_done.md` if the current problem resembles a completed one. Do not write to either.
127	
128	## Summary Pipeline
129	
130	1. Read `task_pending.md` and `task_done.md` for context from prior sessions
131	2. Read the informal proof / blueprint to understand the proof strategy and lemma decomposition
132	3. Introduce helper lemmas (matching the blueprint's structure) in the `.lean` file
133	4. Replace `sorry` placeholders with complete proofs, ensuring the file compiles without errors
134	5. Do not modify protected declarations listed in `archon-protected.yaml`.
135	6. Use Mathlib theorems when possible. Use Web Search when Mathlib lacks referenced results
136	7. Rely on Lean LSP for diagnostics; use `lake env lean <file>` sparingly for final checks
137	8. Log all explorations in `task_results/<your_file>.md`
138	
139	## End-of-session handoff
140	
141	Before you stop (or when you are done with this round of work):
142	
143	1. Write to `task_results/<your_file>.md` with:
144	   - Current result (IN PROGRESS / FAILED) and what you tried
145	   - Any Mathlib lemmas you discovered that are relevant
146	   - Concrete next step for the next session
147	   - Dead-end warnings for approaches that won't work
148	2. Save all file changes (ensure compilation passes, using scoped `sorry` if needed)
149

58	## Current Objectives
59	
60	The dispatcher fans out one prover for the single file listed below.
61	
62	### 1. `AlgebraicJacobian/Cohomology/BasicOpenCech.lean` — eqToHom-blocker breakthrough for step (b), then LSP-per-step (c)..(closure)

117	#### Route (D) — `LinearMap.ext` + key₁ at function level (~15 lines, low–medium risk)
118	
119	**Idea.** Lift the equality out of the `∘ₗ`-application by stating a
120	function-level equality of LinearMaps, then prove it with `LinearMap.ext`
121	+ `intro x` + `key₁` + `map_sum`. The `ext` introduces an explicit `x` whose
122	type Lean elaborates to exactly the codomain of `(∑F).hom`, bypassing the
123	discrimination tree's syntactic-type unification.
124	
125	The cleanest framing: build the equality of three-fold-composed LinearMaps
126	`(Pi.π Z₂ j).hom ∘ₗ eqToHom_hom ∘ₗ (∑F).hom = ∑ i, ((Pi.π Z₂ j).hom ∘ₗ eqToHom_hom ∘ₗ (F i).hom)`,
127	where `F i = (-1)^↑i • Pi.lift (...)` (the actual summand).
128	
129	Concrete tactic chain (the prover should adapt to the actual summand body
130	visible at `lean_goal` L578):
131	
132	```lean
133	-- (D-1) Lift the equality to function level via LinearMap.ext.
134	have routeD :
135	    ∀ (z : ↑(∏ᶜ Z₁ : ModuleCat k)),
136	      (ConcreteCategory.hom (Pi.π Z₂ j))
137	        ((ModuleCat.Hom.hom (eqToHom ⋯) ∘ₗ
138	          ModuleCat.Hom.hom
139	            (∑ i, (-1)^↑i • Pi.lift fun i_1 ↦
140	              Pi.π _ (i_1 ∘ ⇑(SimplexCategory.δ i).toOrderHom) ≫
141	                (toModuleKPresheaf C).map (Pi.lift fun x ↦
142	                  Pi.π (basicOpenCover ↑s₀ ∘ i_1)
143	                    ((SimplexCategory.δ i).toOrderHom x)).op)) z) =
144	      ∑ i,
145	        (ConcreteCategory.hom (Pi.π Z₂ j))
146	          ((ModuleCat.Hom.hom (eqToHom ⋯))
147	            ((ModuleCat.Hom.hom ((-1)^↑i • Pi.lift fun i_1 ↦
148	              Pi.π _ (i_1 ∘ ⇑(SimplexCategory.δ i).toOrderHom) ≫
149	                (toModuleKPresheaf C).map (Pi.lift fun x ↦
150	                  Pi.π (basicOpenCover ↑s₀ ∘ i_1)
151	                    ((SimplexCategory.δ i).toOrderHom x)).op)) z)) := by
152	  intro z
153	  -- The trick: ∘ₗ-unfolding works at the term level via `rfl`-coercion
154	  -- when we explicitly invoke key₁ on the inner application.
155	  -- Lean's elaboration here uses the explicit z's type as the codomain
156	  -- of (∑F).hom, which IS `∏ᶜ Z₂` syntactically once z is named.
157	  rw [show ((ModuleCat.Hom.hom (eqToHom ⋯)) ∘ₗ
158	      (ModuleCat.Hom.hom (∑ i, …))) z =
159	    (ModuleCat.Hom.hom (eqToHom ⋯)) ((ModuleCat.Hom.hom (∑ i, …)) z) from rfl]
160	  -- Now the inner (∑F).hom z is bare; apply key₁.
161	  rw [key₁ (fun i => …) z]
162	  -- Goal: (Pi.π Z₂ j).hom (eqToHom_hom (∑ i, (F i).hom z)) = ∑ i, …
163	  rw [map_sum (ModuleCat.Hom.hom (eqToHom ⋯) : _ →ₗ[k] _)]
164	  rw [map_sum ((Pi.π Z₂ j).hom : _ →ₗ[k] _)]
165	
166	-- (D-2) Apply routeD to both LHS (z := e₁.symm (r•y)) and RHS (z := e₁.symm y).
167	rw [routeD _, routeD _]
168	-- Now both sides are sums; push r • on RHS into the sum.
169	rw [Finset.smul_sum]
170	-- Per-summand congruence.
171	refine Finset.sum_congr rfl ?_
172	intro i _
173	-- Per-summand R-linearity: (Pi.π Z₂ j).hom (eqToHom_hom (F_i.hom (e₁.symm (r•y))))
174	--                       = r • (Pi.π Z₂ j).hom (eqToHom_hom (F_i.hom (e₁.symm y)))
175	-- Continue with steps (d-body) onwards using the iter-090/091 templates.
176	sorry
177	```
178

181	  bound by `intro` (NOT a free metavariable as in iter-093 attempts). The
182	  prover should test this hypothesis FIRST via `lean_multi_attempt` at L578
183	  before committing.
184	- If the inline `rw [show … from rfl]` STILL fails (worst case), try
185	  `change … = …` with the explicit unfolded form, or `simp only [LinearMap.comp_apply]`
186	  AFTER the `intro z` (the elaboration context differs from the goal at L578).
187	- The actual summand body at L578 may differ slightly from the recipe; use
188	  `lean_goal` and copy the exact form.
189	- After (D-1) is proved, the `rw [routeD _]` substitution should fire cleanly

127	## Forbidden shortcuts (documented once)
128	
129	Standing rules:
130	- No new `axiom` declarations.
131	- No constant/vacuous shortcuts for protected definitions.
132	- No `≃` / `≃+` where `≃ₗ[k]` is required.
133	- Use `theorem` (not `instance`) for consumers taking explicit non-synthesisable arguments.
134	- Avoid `Scheme.` short-name prefix inside `namespace AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme`.
135	- Defining `Jacobian C := 𝟙_ _` *unconditionally* — forbidden.
136	- Plan-agent must not pre-verify candidate proof bodies via `lean_run_code` / `lean_multi_attempt`; no chains of thin helper extraction across iterations.
137	- Future code opening `MonObj` must avoid `ι` as a binder name (scoped Mathlib notation for `GrpObj.inv`).
138	- **iter-086**: No helper with a universally-false signature may be introduced even with a `sorry` body. Example violation (iter-085): `lemma foo (S : ShortComplex ...) : S.Exact := sorry` claims every short complex is exact. When the genuine `iff`-form is not closeable, leave the inline `sorry` rather than displacing it via a `sorry`-bodied helper.
139	- **iter-086**: No 80+-line in-code comment blocks documenting dead-end tactics; if the lane stalls, write a SHORT task report and escalate to refactor rather than expanding the inline scaffolding.
140	- **iter-087**: When extracting an inline `have` to a top-level decl via refactor, the extracted signature MUST be concrete to the call site's specialized context. iter-087 first-pass `cechCofaceMap_pi_smul` had a universally-false signature; specialize pass caught it.
141	- **iter-089**: When committing a multi-step tactic chain (here S6 = a,b,c,…h) without LSP feedback, do NOT wrap the whole chain in `first | <chain> | sorry` — that loses the "deepest committed step" signal needed for next-iter re-scoping. Commit each step linearly and put the trailing `sorry` after the deepest committed step.
142	- **iter-090**: For multi-step tactic chains under LSP-unavailable conditions, prefer universal `simp only [map_sum]` over direct `rw [foo.map_sum, …]` for AddMonoidHomClass distribution — `simp only` tolerates HOU pattern variants and iterates through nested wrappers (`eqToHom`, `Function.comp`, etc.). Also: when both sides of an equation are sums but the RHS has `r • ∑` shape, prefer forward-direction `simp only [Finset.smul_sum]` BEFORE `Finset.sum_congr` over the original end-of-chain `rw [← Finset.smul_sum]` — mathematically equivalent, structurally cleaner. The per-step fallback ladder is now refined to per-step granularity (each step in the chain is its own commit point).
143	- **iter-092**: `sorry_analyzer.py` counts syntactic `sorry` tokens but does NOT verify compilation. When mathlib was missing in the sandbox (iter-086 → iter-091), only `sorry_analyzer.py` ran for verification, and false positives accumulated. **Plan-agent verification step: ALWAYS run `lake build <target>` or `lean_diagnostic_messages` as part of the verification pass; do NOT trust `sorry_analyzer.py` alone for compilation status.** When the sandbox is missing mathlib, `lake exe cache get` from project root + `$HOME/.elan/bin` on PATH fetches it; this works in iter-092+. The prover under LSP-unavailable conditions should NOT commit tactic chains beyond a single step — the iter-091 bare-commit-without-LSP pattern stacks unverified work and produces false-positive sorry counts.
144	- **iter-092**: `letI x : T := v` in CONCLUSION position of a `theorem ... :  ... := by ...` is silently zeta-reduced AWAY during elaboration if `x` doesn't appear explicitly in the rest of the conclusion. The instance synthesis hint provided by the letI is BAKED INTO downstream `•`/`*`/`⬝`-style instance uses, but the let-binder `x` does NOT survive. `intro` cannot name what isn't there. **Idiom for the pattern "introduce instances in the statement so the conclusion typechecks, then access them by name in the body":** move the `letI` block from conclusion-position to body-position (right after `intro` of the let-binders that the letI bodies depend on). Both forms compute the same baked-in instance; the body-position form keeps the name in scope for downstream tactics.
145	- **iter-093**: When `simp only [lem]` / `rw [lem]` fails with "made no progress" or "Did not find an occurrence of the pattern" on a goal that visibly contains the LHS of `lem`, the cause is usually HOU pattern matching failure (the summand or closure body references the bound index in two or more positions). Standard escalation ladder: (1) try `simp_rw [lem]` first — Lean's `simp_rw` is more tolerant of HOU patterns than `simp only`; (2) if `simp_rw` also fails, build the equation manually via an explicit `AddMonoidHom` / `RingHom` / `LinearMap` and apply `map_sum` / `map_mul` / `map_smul` — these `map_*` lemmas operate on the structure homomorphism rather than via discrimination-tree HOU; (3) as a last resort, use `Finset.cons_induction` with per-step `Finset.sum_cons` + `map_add` (or the relevant additivity lemma) + IH.
146	- **iter-094**: `LinearMap.comp_apply` (`rfl`-lemma: `(f ∘ₗ g) x = f (g x)`) does NOT fire if `g`'s codomain and `f`'s source are def-equal but NOT syntactically equal. Symptoms: `simp only [LinearMap.comp_apply]` reports "no progress"; `rw [LinearMap.comp_apply]` reports "pattern `(?f ∘ₗ ?g) ?x` not found"; `rw [show (f ∘ₗ g) x = f (g x) from rfl]` reports same. Cause: the discrimination tree's unification fails to identify two def-equal-but-syntactically-distinct types (e.g. `∏ᶜ Z₂` vs `ModuleCat.of k (Pi Z₂)`). **Escalation ladder**: (1) try `LinearMap.ext` to lift the equality OUT of the `∘ₗ`-application — at the `intro x` binder, Lean elaborates the codomain explicitly, bypassing the discrimination tree; (2) eliminate the source of the mismatch (e.g., drop the simp lemma that introduced the def-equal unfolding from the simp set; replace with explicit `Eq.mpr` cast); (3) restructure to push the offending wrapping outside the sum at a higher level of abstraction (categorical, not LinearMap).

587	/-- Iter-060 (Phase A step 6 / Path 2 — **substantive Čech acyclicity attempt**):
588	the basic-open cover of an affine open `U` by a spanning subset `s ⊆ Γ(C.left, U)`
589	gives a Čech-acyclic cover for the `ModuleCat k`-valued structure sheaf
590	`toModuleKSheaf C`. This is the Stacks 01ED Čech-acyclicity theorem for affine
591	schemes in our setting.
592	
593	**Mathematical content (Stacks 01ED route).** For each positive degree `n`,
594	exactness of the Čech cochain at degree `n` is a *local* property on the
595	spanning set `s`. Localising the Čech complex at any `f ∈ s` exhibits the
596	slice over `D(f)` as a Čech complex of an affine cover whose terminal vertex
597	`D(f) → D(f)` supplies an *extra degeneracy* (Mathlib's
598	`FormalCoproduct.extraDegeneracyCech`). By
599	`SimplicialObject.Augmented.ExtraDegeneracy.homotopyEquiv` the augmented
600	chain complex of the slice is homotopy equivalent to the constant complex on
601	the augmentation, hence exact in positive degrees. Re-globalising via
602	`exact_of_isLocalized_span` (Mathlib `RingTheory/LocalProperties/Exactness.lean`
603	L173) yields exactness of the *unlocalised* Čech complex at degree `n`.
604	
605	The structural skeleton is:
606	* reduce `Subsingleton (cechCohomology n)` to `ExactAt (cechCochain) n` via
607	  `cechCohomology_subsingleton_of_cechCochain_exactAt`;
608	* the substantive `ExactAt n` is decomposed into three substeps tracked by
609	  separate `sorry`s, each annotated with the missing computational step.
610	
611	**Transient sorries (iter-060)** — explicitly endorsed by the iteration
612	directive; each sorry names a substep. The substeps are:
613	  (a) **local exactness at `D(f)`** (extra-degeneracy invocation): given the
614	      cover-of-`D(f)` slice, build the augmented Čech simplicial object,
615	      apply `FormalCoproduct.extraDegeneracyCech` to obtain an
616	      `ExtraDegeneracy`, then transport via
617	      `ExtraDegeneracy.homotopyEquiv` and op-passage `CochainComplex.opEquivalence`
618	      to get cochain-level exactness in positive degrees;
619	  (b) **localised-Čech identification**: the localisation at `f` of the
620	      degree-`n` Čech cochain is the degree-`n` cochain of the cover-of-`D(f)`
621	      Čech complex, via iter-057+ identifications chained with
622	      `IsLocalizedModule.map_exact`;
623	  (c) **local-to-global** via `exact_of_isLocalized_span`: the assembled
624	      family of per-`f` local exactnesses lifts to global exactness.
625	
626	The decomposition follows §"Branch A — Čech acyclicity" of
627	`analogies/cech-koszul-precedent.md`. -/
628	theorem basicOpenCover_isCechAcyclicCover_toModuleKSheaf
629	    {k : Type u} [Field k] {C : Over (Spec (.of k))}
630	    {U : TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat} (hU : IsAffineOpen U)
631	    (s : Set Γ(C.left, U)) (hs : Ideal.span s = ⊤) :
632	    IsCechAcyclicCover (toModuleKSheaf C)
633	        (basicOpenCover (C := C) (U := U) s) where
634	  subsingleton_cechCohomology n hn := by
635	    -- Step 0 (structural): reduce `Subsingleton (cechCohomology n)` to
636	    -- `ExactAt n` of the underlying Čech cochain complex via the iter-060
637	    -- helper `cechCohomology_subsingleton_of_cechCochain_exactAt`.
638	    refine cechCohomology_subsingleton_of_cechCochain_exactAt (n := n) ?_
639	    -- The goal is now
640	    --   `(cechCochain C (toModuleKSheaf C) (basicOpenCover s)).ExactAt n`.
641	    -- Iter-061 decomposition: the Stacks 01ED extra-degeneracy + local-to-global
642	    -- argument has three substeps tracked by separate labelled `sorry`s.
643	    --
644	    -- ===========================================================================
645	    -- Substep (a) — Extra-degeneracy on the slice cover of `D(f)`.
646	    -- ===========================================================================
647	    -- For each `f ∈ s`, the slice cover of `D(f)` by the family
648	    --   `{basicOpenCover s f' ⊓ D(f)}_{f' ∈ s}`
649	    -- contains `D(f) ⊓ D(f) = D(f)` itself (at index `f' = f`), so the
650	    -- terminal vertex maps to `D(f)` via the identity section
651	    -- `D(f) → D(f)`. By `FormalCoproduct.extraDegeneracyCech`
652	    -- (`Mathlib/CategoryTheory/Limits/FormalCoproducts/ExtraDegeneracy.lean` L92),
653	    -- the augmented Čech simplicial object on this slice has an
654	    -- `ExtraDegeneracy`. By `SimplicialObject.Augmented.ExtraDegeneracy.homotopyEquiv`
655	    -- (`Mathlib/AlgebraicTopology/ExtraDegeneracy.lean` L328), the associated
656	    -- alternating-face-map chain complex is homotopy-equivalent to the
657	    -- constant complex on the augmentation. Op-passage through
658	    -- `CochainComplex.opEquivalence` then yields cochain-level exactness in
659	    -- positive degrees. We state substep (a) as a concrete `ExactAt n`
660	    -- claim on the slice cover Čech cochain complex:
661	    have h_a : ∀ (f : s),
662	        (cechCochain C (toModuleKSheaf C)
663	            (fun (f' : s) => basicOpenCover (C := C) (U := U) s f' ⊓
664	              C.left.basicOpen (f : Γ(C.left, U)))).ExactAt n := by
665	      intro f
666	      -- The terminal vertex of the slice cover is at `f' = f`:
667	      --   `basicOpenCover s f ⊓ C.left.basicOpen f.1 = D(f) ⊓ D(f) = D(f)`.
668	      -- Plug into `FormalCoproduct.extraDegeneracyCech` with `i₀ := f`,
669	      -- extract `homotopyEquiv`, op-pass, read off `ExactAt n` for `0 < n`.
670	      sorry  -- substep (a) infrastructure: augmented Čech simplicial object
671	             -- + extraDegeneracyCech + homotopyEquiv + CochainComplex.opEquivalence
672	    -- ===========================================================================
673	    -- Substep (b) — Localised-Čech identification.
674	    -- ===========================================================================
675	    -- For each `f ∈ s`, the slice cover Čech cochain complex above is
676	    -- isomorphic (in `ModuleCat k`) to the localisation at `f` of the
677	    -- original Čech cochain complex `K`. Cochain-factor-wise:
678	    --   `(slice cover).X^n = ∏_x F(⨅ k, basicOpenCover s (x k) ⊓ D(f))`
679	    --                      = `∏_x F(D(f · ∏ k, (x k).1))`              (Stacks identity)
680	    --                      = `∏_x Localization.Away (f · ∏ k, (x k).1) Γ(C.left, U)`
681	    --                      = `∏_x Γ(C.left, ⨅ k, basicOpenCover s (x k))[1/f]`
682	    --                      = `K.X^n [1/f]`.
683	    -- The first equality is iter-057 (`basicOpenCover_finset_inf'_eq_basicOpen_prod`)
684	    -- applied to the augmented index `{f} ∪ image(x)`. The second is
685	    -- iter-059 (`basicOpenCover_finset_inf'_isLocalization`). The third is
686	    -- the universal property of localisation (transitivity of localisation:
687	    -- `R[1/(f·g)] = R[1/g][1/f]` when `f` is also among the generators).
688	    -- The differential identification uses `IsLocalizedModule.map_exact`
689	    -- (`Mathlib/Algebra/Module/LocalizedModule/Exact.lean` L56) to transport
690	    -- the cochain-level identification to the slice differential.
691	    --
692	    -- ===========================================================================
693	    -- Substep (c) — Local-to-global via `exact_of_isLocalized_span`.
694	    -- ===========================================================================
695	    -- The combined data from substeps (a) and (b) — per-`f` exactness at
696	    -- degree `n` of the slice cover Čech complex, which by substep (b) is
697	    -- the localisation of `K.sc n` — fits the hypothesis of
698	    -- `exact_of_isLocalized_span s hs ...`
699	    -- (`Mathlib/RingTheory/LocalProperties/Exactness.lean` L173) viewing
700	    -- the cochain factors as `Γ(C.left, U)`-modules through their natural
701	    -- module structure. The conclusion is `Function.Exact ⇑(K.sc n).f ⇑(K.sc n).g`,
702	    -- which converts back to `K.ExactAt n` via
703	    -- `ShortComplex.ShortExact.moduleCat_exact_iff_function_exact` and
704	    -- `HomologicalComplex.exactAt_iff`.
705	    --
706	    -- This combined assembly remains as a single transient sorry: it
707	    -- requires constructing the cochain-level localisation iso (substep (b)
708	    -- ≈ a `noncomputable def` for the localised Čech short complex plus
709	    -- its identification iso) and routing the ring-base mismatch
710	    -- (`(K.sc n).f.hom` is `k`-linear; `exact_of_isLocalized_span` consumes
711	    -- `Γ(C.left, U)`-linear maps — they coincide as set-functions, but the
712	    -- type-level reconciliation is non-trivial).
713	    --
714	    -- For iter-061, substep (a)'s claim is concretely stated above and
715	    -- substep (b)+(c)'s assembly remains as the second labelled sorry below.
716	    -- We perform the mechanical conversion `K.ExactAt n ↔ Function.Exact ⇑f ⇑g`
717	    -- before the sorry, so the remaining content is precisely the
718	    -- "transport per-f exactness through the localisation iso then apply
719	    -- the local-to-global principle" assembly step.
720	    rw [HomologicalComplex.exactAt_iff,
721	        ShortComplex.ShortExact.moduleCat_exact_iff_function_exact]
722	    -- Goal:
723	    --   `Function.Exact ⇑(ConcreteCategory.hom ((K).sc n).f)
724	    --                   ⇑(ConcreteCategory.hom ((K).sc n).g)`
725	    -- where `K := cechCochain C (toModuleKSheaf C) (basicOpenCover s)`.
726	    -- ===========================================================================
727	    -- Iter-062 substantive scaffolding (a): convert `h_a` into `Function.Exact` form.
728	    -- ===========================================================================
729	    -- The same `HomologicalComplex.exactAt_iff` +
730	    -- `ShortComplex.ShortExact.moduleCat_exact_iff_function_exact` chain that
731	    -- converts our goal also converts each per-`f` slice-cover `ExactAt n`
732	    -- hypothesis into a per-`f` `Function.Exact` statement at the
733	    -- function-level. This is closed under `← rw` (no new sorry).
734	    have h_a_fun : ∀ (f : s),
735	        Function.Exact
736	          ⇑(ConcreteCategory.hom (HomologicalComplex.sc
737	              (cechCochain C (toModuleKSheaf C)
738	                (fun (f' : s) => basicOpenCover (C := C) (U := U) s f' ⊓
739	                  C.left.basicOpen (f : Γ(C.left, U)))) n).f)
740	          ⇑(ConcreteCategory.hom (HomologicalComplex.sc
741	              (cechCochain C (toModuleKSheaf C)
742	                (fun (f' : s) => basicOpenCover (C := C) (U := U) s f' ⊓
743	                  C.left.basicOpen (f : Γ(C.left, U)))) n).g) := by
744	      intro f
745	      rw [← ShortComplex.ShortExact.moduleCat_exact_iff_function_exact,
746	          ← HomologicalComplex.exactAt_iff]
747	      exact h_a f
748	    -- ===========================================================================
749	    -- Iter-062 substantive scaffolding (b): outline the local-to-global route.
750	    -- ===========================================================================
751	    -- The closure of the remaining goal proceeds via Mathlib's local-to-global
752	    -- principle for exactness `exact_of_isLocalized_span` applied to the
753	    -- ring `R := Γ(C.left, U)`, the spanning subset `s ⊆ R`, and the
754	    -- `R`-linear maps `(K.sc n).f.hom` and `(K.sc n).g.hom` (treated as
755	    -- `R`-linear via the `Module R ((K.sc n).X i)`-structure obtained from
756	    -- the cochain factor's natural `Γ(C.left, ⋂_x …)`-module structure
757	    -- precomposed with the algebra map `R → Γ(C.left, ⋂_x …)`).
758	    -- The required input is, for each `f ∈ s`, a `Function.Exact`
759	    -- statement on the localised cochain factors at `f`. By the
760	    -- cochain-factor identification chain (iter-057 + iter-058 + iter-059
761	    -- + Mathlib `Localization.localizationLocalization` + product-localisation
762	    -- commutation), the localised `(K.sc n).X i [1/f]` identifies with the
763	    -- slice-cover Čech `.X i` at the same degree, and consequently the
764	    -- localised differential identifies with the slice-cover differential.
765	    -- This identification + `IsLocalizedModule.map_exact` transports
766	    -- `h_a_fun f` (which is the slice-cover Čech `Function.Exact` at
767	    -- degree `n`) to `Function.Exact` of the localised differential.
768	    -- The infinite-set subtlety (`s` is `Set`, not `Finset`) is sidestepped
769	    -- because `Ideal.span s = ⊤` already implies a *finite* subset spans
770	    -- the unit ideal — `exact_of_isLocalized_span` only consumes the per-`r`
771	    -- localised exactness data and the spanning hypothesis, not finiteness
772	    -- of `s` directly.
773	    -- ===========================================================================
774	    -- Substep (b1) — install the `Module Γ(C.left, U)` instances on each
775	    -- cochain factor `(K.sc n).X i`.
776	    -- ===========================================================================
777	    -- The cochain factor `K.X i = ∏_{x : Fin (i+1) → s} Γ(C.left, ⋂_k 𝒰(x k))`
778	    -- carries a `Γ(C.left, U)`-module structure pointwise, since each
779	    -- `Γ(C.left, V)` for `V ≤ U` is a `Γ(C.left, U)`-algebra via the
780	    -- restriction map (iter-058's `basicOpenCover_finset_inf'_le` supplies
781	    -- the inclusion `V ≤ U`, iter-006's `algebraSection` supplies the
782	    -- algebra structure on each factor).
783	    --
784	    -- ===========================================================================
785	    -- Substep (b2) — exhibit `slice_K.X i` as `IsLocalizedModule.Away f.1`
786	    -- of `(K.sc n).X i` for each `f ∈ s`.
787	    -- ===========================================================================
788	    -- The cochain-factor identification chain (iter-057 + iter-058 + iter-059
789	    -- + Mathlib `Localization.localizationLocalization` + product-localisation
790	    -- commutation) gives
791	    --   `slice_K.X i = ∏_x Γ(C.left, ⋂_k 𝒰_slice(x k))`
792	    --                = `∏_x Γ(C.left, ⋂_k 𝒰(x k) ⊓ D(f))`
793	    --                = `∏_x Γ(C.left, ⋂_k 𝒰(x k))[1/f]`        (iter-058 + iter-059)
794	    --                = `(∏_x Γ(C.left, ⋂_k 𝒰(x k)))[1/f]`     (product-loc commutation)
795	    --                = `(K.X i)[1/f]`.
796	    -- The pointwise localisation map (component-wise restriction map
797	    -- `Γ(C.left, V) → Γ(C.left, V')` for `V' = V ⊓ D(f) ≤ V`) is the canonical
798	    -- localisation map and an `IsLocalizedModule.Away f.1` instance.
799	    --
800	    -- ===========================================================================
801	    -- Substep (c) — apply `exact_of_isLocalized_span s hs ... h_a_fun`.
802	    -- ===========================================================================
803	    -- With the data from (b1) and (b2), `exact_of_isLocalized_span` packages
804	    -- the per-`f` exactness `h_a_fun f` into the global `Function.Exact`
805	    -- conclusion. The remaining work is purely the unification of the per-`f`
806	    -- input shape with the conclusion of `IsLocalizedModule.map ... F`
807	    -- via the iso from (b2) and `IsLocalizedModule.map_exact`.
808	    -- ===========================================================================
809	    -- Iter-063 progress: explicit finite-subspanning extraction + obstruction
810	    -- diagnosis.
811	    -- ===========================================================================
812	    -- The localisation-commutes-with-products subtlety identified by the
813	    -- iter-061 prover (Attempt 3) is genuine and severe: for the cochain
814	    -- factor `K.X i = ∏ᶜ_{x : Fin (i+1) → s} P.obj (op (∏ᶜ_k 𝒰(x k)))` in
815	    -- `ModuleCat k` (where `P` is the underlying presheaf of `toModuleKSheaf C`),
816	    -- the index set `Fin (i+1) → s` is *infinite* when `s` is infinite, and
817	    -- `LocalizedModule (powers f) (∏ᶜ_x M_x) ≄ ∏ᶜ_x LocalizedModule (powers f) M_x`
818	    -- in general for infinite products. So `slice_K.X i = ∏_x Γ(V_x ⊓ D(f))`
819	    -- is NOT literally `(K.X i)[1/f]` — the natural map between them is
820	    -- *not* an `IsLocalizedModule.Away f.1`. Iter-062's substep (b2) plan
821	    -- ("install `IsLocalizedModule.Away f.1` instances on the product")
822	    -- requires reducing to a finite index-set first.
823	    --
824	    -- Iter-063 lands the finite-subspanning extraction as a structural fact
825	    -- inside the proof: `Ideal.span s = ⊤` implies the existence of a
826	    -- *finite* subset `s₀ ⊆ s` with `Ideal.span ↑s₀ = ⊤` via Mathlib's
827	    -- `Ideal.span_eq_top_iff_finite`. The remaining substep work uses `s₀`
828	    -- (a `Finset`, hence `Fin (i+1) → ↑s₀` is finite) to install the
829	    -- product-localisation commutation. The transport step from `s₀`-cover
830	    -- exactness to `s`-cover exactness is itself non-trivial: it requires
831	    -- a Čech-complex refinement comparison (`s₀ ⊆ s` ⇒ Čech-cohomology
832	    -- equality), which Mathlib does not currently expose directly but can
833	    -- be derived from the universal property of products via `Pi.eqOfPi`
834	    -- + the per-component (cofinal) refinement.
835	    have hs_fin : ∃ s' : Finset Γ(C.left, U), (↑s' : Set Γ(C.left, U)) ⊆ s ∧
836	        Ideal.span (↑s' : Set Γ(C.left, U)) = ⊤ :=
837	      (Ideal.span_eq_top_iff_finite (s := s)).mp hs
838	    -- ===========================================================================
839	    -- Substep (b1-b2-c) merged with the iter-063 finite-subspanning data.
840	    -- ===========================================================================
841	    -- The route now has three components: (i) Čech-cohomology refinement
842	    -- transport `s → s₀` (using `hs_fin`); (ii) substep (b2) at the finite
843	    -- cover `s₀` where the product-localisation commutation succeeds; and
844	    -- (iii) `exact_of_isLocalized_span` on `s₀` together with the per-`f`
845	    -- slice-cover exactness `h_a_fun ⟨f, hs_fin.choose_spec.1 hf⟩` for
846	    -- `f ∈ s₀ ⊆ s`.
847	    --
848	    -- The Čech-refinement transport (i) is the new iter-064+ work; it has
849	    -- the form
850	    -- `Function.Exact (K(s).sc n).f (K(s).sc n).g ↔
851	    --    Function.Exact (K(s₀).sc n).f (K(s₀).sc n).g`
852	    -- under `hs_fin`. A natural framing: there is a *cochain-complex map*
853	    -- `K(s) → K(s₀)` (induced by the cofinal inclusion `s₀ ⊆ s` and the
854	    -- product universal property), and exactness transports along it
855	    -- iff one shows the map is a "Čech equivalence" — equivalently, by
856	    -- the lattice-supremum agreement `⨆ basicOpenCover s = U = ⨆ basicOpenCover s₀`,
857	    -- the two covers compute the same Čech cohomology via the
858	    -- `cechCohomology` carrier.
859	    -- ===========================================================================
860	    -- Iter-064: component (i) scaffolding — Čech-cohomology refinement transport.
861	    -- ===========================================================================
862	    -- Extract the finite subspanning set `s₀` and its properties.
863	    obtain ⟨s₀, h_sub, h_top⟩ := hs_fin
864	    -- Introduce shorthand for the two Čech cochain complexes.
865	    let K := cechCochain C (toModuleKSheaf C) (basicOpenCover s)
866	    let K₀ := cechCochain C (toModuleKSheaf C)
867	      (basicOpenCover (C := C) (U := U) (↑s₀ : Set Γ(C.left, U)))
868	    -- The inclusion `↑s₀ ⊆ s` induces a cochain-complex map `K → K₀` by
869	    -- precomposing multi-indices.  Exactness transports across this map because
870	    -- both covers generate the same sieve on `U` (`⨆ basicOpenCover s = U =
871	    -- ⨆ basicOpenCover ↑s₀`).  The transport is stated as a `have` below;
872	    -- closing it requires either a direct homological-algebra argument
873	    -- (quasi-isomorphism induced by the refinement) or a reduction to the
874	    -- sheaf-theoretic fact that Čech cohomology depends only on the generated
875	    -- sieve.  Mathlib does not yet expose this comparison directly.
876	    have h_transport :
877	      Function.Exact
878	        ⇑(ConcreteCategory.hom (HomologicalComplex.sc K₀ n).f)
879	        ⇑(ConcreteCategory.hom (HomologicalComplex.sc K₀ n).g) →
880	      Function.Exact
881	        ⇑(ConcreteCategory.hom (HomologicalComplex.sc K n).f)
882	        ⇑(ConcreteCategory.hom (HomologicalComplex.sc K n).g) := by
883	      -- =========================================================================
884	      -- Iter-065 scaffolding: Čech-cohomology refinement transport `s → s₀`.
885	      -- =========================================================================
886	      intro h_exact_K₀
887	      -- Step 1: the inclusion `↑s₀ ↪ s` induces a map of index sets
888	      -- `Fin (i+1) → ↑s₀ → Fin (i+1) → s` for each degree `i`.  By the universal
889	      -- property of the categorical product, this gives a projection map
890	      -- `π_i : K.X i → K₀.X i` in `ModuleCat k` (project onto the smaller index
891	      -- set).  These assemble into a cochain-complex map `π : K → K₀` because
892	      -- the Čech differential is natural in the index set.
893	      --
894	      -- **Iter-065 concrete construction** of `π`.  We assemble it through the
895	      -- Mathlib API:
896	      --   1. The inclusion `↑s₀ ⊆ s` gives a `FormalCoproduct`-morphism
897	      --      `g_FC : ⟨↑s₀, basicOpenCover ↑s₀⟩ ⟶ ⟨s, basicOpenCover s⟩`
898	      --      (function `j ↦ ⟨j.1, h_sub j.2⟩` on indices, identity on objects
899	      --      since `basicOpenCover s ⟨j.1, _⟩ = C.left.basicOpen j.1 =
900	      --      basicOpenCover ↑s₀ j`).
901	      --   2. `Limits.FormalCoproduct.cechFunctor` applied to `g_FC` produces a
902	      --      morphism of simplicial objects in `FormalCoproduct (Opens _)`.
903	      --   3. `cosimplicialObjectFunctor` precomposes with `_.rightOp`, hence is
904	      --      *contravariant* in the simplicial object.  Applying it via
905	      --      `whiskeringLeft` yields a natural transformation
906	      --      `cosimplicialObjectFunctor (mk s _).cech ⟶
907	      --       cosimplicialObjectFunctor (mk ↑s₀ _).cech`.
908	      --   4. Whiskering with `alternatingCofaceMapComplex` and evaluating at
909	      --      the underlying presheaf of `toModuleKSheaf C` gives the desired
910	      --      cochain-complex morphism `K ⟶ K₀`.
911	      let g_FC :
912	          (Limits.FormalCoproduct.mk (↑s₀ : Set Γ(C.left, U))
913	              (basicOpenCover (C := C) (U := U) (↑s₀ : Set Γ(C.left, U)))) ⟶
914	          (Limits.FormalCoproduct.mk s
915	              (basicOpenCover (C := C) (U := U) s)) :=
916	        { f := fun j => ⟨j.1, h_sub j.2⟩
917	          φ := fun _ => 𝟙 _ }
918	      let g_simp :
919	          (Limits.FormalCoproduct.mk (↑s₀ : Set Γ(C.left, U))
920	              (basicOpenCover (C := C) (U := U)
921	                (↑s₀ : Set Γ(C.left, U)))).cech ⟶
922	          (Limits.FormalCoproduct.mk s
923	              (basicOpenCover (C := C) (U := U) s)).cech :=
924	        Limits.FormalCoproduct.cechFunctor.map g_FC
925	      let π : HomologicalComplex.Hom K K₀ :=
926	        ((Functor.whiskerLeft (Limits.FormalCoproduct.evalOp _ (ModuleCat.{u} k))
927	            ((Functor.whiskeringLeft _ _ _).map g_simp.rightOp)) ◫
928	          𝟙 (AlgebraicTopology.alternatingCofaceMapComplex (ModuleCat.{u} k))).app
929	          ((sheafToPresheaf _ _).obj (toModuleKSheaf C))
930	      -- Step 2: each component `π_i` is a split surjection in `ModuleCat k`.
931	      -- The splitting is given by extending a function `Fin (i+1) → ↑s₀` to
932	      -- `Fin (i+1) → s` by choosing arbitrary values on the complement of `↑s₀`
933	      -- (possible because `s` is non-empty, as `Ideal.span s = ⊤`).
934	      --
935	      -- Iter-066: the categorical content is captured by
936	      -- `splitEpi_pi_lift_of_injective` (defined above), which says a product
937	      -- projection induced by an injection of index sets is split in
938	      -- `ModuleCat k`. The remaining obligation is to identify `π.f i` as such
939	      -- a product projection — namely
940	      -- `Pi.lift (fun j' : Fin(i+1) → ↑s₀ => Pi.π M (g_FC.f ∘ j'))` for the
941	      -- family `M : (Fin(i+1) → s) → ModuleCat k` given by
942	      -- `j ↦ F.obj (op (∏ᶜ_a basicOpenCover s (j a)))`. From the simp lemmas of
943	      -- `evalOp` (`Mathlib/CategoryTheory/Limits/FormalCoproducts/Basic.lean` L383),
944	      -- of `cechFunctor` (`.../FormalCoproducts/Cech.lean` L194), and of
945	      -- `alternatingCofaceMapComplex`, `π.f i` reduces to exactly this form
946	      -- (the `≫ F.map (𝟙).op` factor collapses by `F.map_id` since
947	      -- `(powerMap g_FC (Fin (i+1))).φ j' = 𝟙` for our `g_FC`).
948	      --
949	      -- Decomposing into named subobligations:
950	      have h_inj : Function.Injective (g_FC.f) := fun a b hab => by
951	        -- g_FC.f j = ⟨j.1, h_sub j.2⟩ ∈ s; injective because Subtype.val is.
952	        have : (g_FC.f a).1 = (g_FC.f b).1 := congrArg Subtype.val hab
953	        exact Subtype.ext this
954	      have h_inj' (i : ℕ) :
955	          Function.Injective (fun (j' : Fin (i + 1) → ↑s₀) => g_FC.f ∘ j') :=
956	        fun a b hab => funext (fun x => h_inj (congrFun hab x))
957	      -- The actual identification of `π.f i` with the product projection (and
958	      -- thus the SplitEpi) remains to be carried out. The helper
959	      -- `splitEpi_pi_lift_of_injective` reduces the work to this identification
960	      -- step. See `task_results/BasicOpenCech.lean.md` for the iter-066
961	      -- analysis of the simp lemmas needed.
962	      have h_π_split (i : ℕ) : SplitEpi (π.f i) := by
963	        -- Approach: use convert with the helper, then close the equality subgoal
964	        -- via simp lemmas for evalOp, cechFunctor, and alternatingCofaceMapComplex.
965	        convert splitEpi_pi_lift_of_injective _ (fun j' => g_FC.f ∘ j') (h_inj' i) using 2
966	        · -- After convert, the goal is π.f i = Pi.lift (...).  Simplify the
967	          -- left-hand side using the simps lemmas for evalOp, cechFunctor,
968	          -- and alternatingCofaceMapComplex, then unfold g_simp and use the
969	          -- powerMap simps.  The remaining identity morphisms are eliminated
970	          -- pointwise after congr+funext.
971	          simp [π]
972	          rw [show g_simp = FormalCoproduct.cechFunctor.map g_FC by rfl]
973	          simp [FormalCoproduct.cechFunctor_map_app, FormalCoproduct.powerMap_f,
974	            FormalCoproduct.powerMap_φ]
975	          dsimp [g_FC]
976	          congr
977	          funext i_1
978	          erw [Limits.Pi.map_id, CategoryTheory.op_id, CategoryTheory.Functor.map_id,
979	            CategoryTheory.Category.comp_id]
980	      -- Step 3: the kernel of `π` at degree `i` is the product of the factors
981	      -- indexed by `x : Fin (i+1) → s` that are *not* in the image of
982	      -- `Fin (i+1) → ↑s₀`.  The kernel complex is acyclic in positive degrees
983	      -- because it is a Čech complex of a cover that is a refinement of a cover
984	      -- with a terminal object (the extra-degeneracy argument applies to the
985	      -- kernel once `s` is reduced modulo `↑s₀`).  Alternatively, one can use
986	      -- the sheaf-theoretic fact that Čech cohomology depends only on the
987	      -- generated sieve, and both `s` and `s₀` generate the same sieve `U`.
988	      --
989	      -- Step 4: from the short exact sequence of complexes
990	      -- `0 → ker(π) → K → K₀ → 0` and exactness of `K₀` at degree `n`, the
991	      -- long exact sequence in cohomology gives exactness of `K` at degree `n`.
992	      -- A direct diagram-chase argument (or `HomologicalComplex.homologyMap`)
993	      -- closes the goal.
994	      sorry
995	    -- ===========================================================================
996	    -- Iter-064: component (ii) scaffolding — `exact_of_isLocalized_span` on `s₀`.
997	    -- ===========================================================================
998	    -- We now prove exactness of `K₀` using the finite-spanning local-to-global
999	    -- principle.  Because `s₀` is finite, the cochain factors of `K₀` are
1000	    -- *finite* products, so product-localisation commutation holds and
1001	    -- `IsLocalizedModule.Away f.1` instances can be installed.
1002	    have h_K₀_exact : Function.Exact
1003	        ⇑(ConcreteCategory.hom (HomologicalComplex.sc K₀ n).f)
1004	        ⇑(ConcreteCategory.hom (HomologicalComplex.sc K₀ n).g) := by
1005	      -- Step 1: install `Module Γ(C.left, U)` instances on each cochain factor
1006	      -- of `K₀`.  Each factor `Γ(C.left, V)` for `V ≤ U` is a
1007	      -- `Γ(C.left, U)`-algebra via the restriction map; the finite product
1008	      -- inherits the pointwise `Γ(C.left, U)`-module structure.
1009	      --
1010	      -- Step 2: for each `f ∈ ↑s₀`, the slice cover Čech complex at `f`
1011	      -- (indexed by `↑s₀`) is exact in degree `n` by the same extra-degeneracy
1012	      -- argument as substep (a): the cover of `D(f)` contains `D(f)` itself
1013	      -- (at index `f`), so the augmented complex contracts.  This is stated
1014	      -- as `h_a₀` below.
1015	      have h_a₀ : ∀ (f : ↑s₀),
1016	          (cechCochain C (toModuleKSheaf C)
1017	            (fun (f' : ↑s₀) => basicOpenCover (C := C) (U := U) (↑s₀ : Set Γ(C.left, U)) f' ⊓
1018	              C.left.basicOpen (f : Γ(C.left, U)))).ExactAt n := by
1019	        intro f

todos.content: Read current L578 goal state via lean_goal

import Mathlib
example {R : Type*} [Ring R] {M N P : ModuleCat R} (f : M ⟶ N) (g : N ⟶ P) :

import Mathlib
open CategoryTheory

example (R : Type) [Ring R] (M N P : ModuleCat R) (f : M ⟶ N) (g : N ⟶ P) (z : M) :
    ModuleCat.Hom.hom (f ≫ g) z = (ConcreteCategory.hom (f ≫ g)) z := rfl

example (R : Type) [Ring R] (M N P : ModuleCat R) (f : M ⟶ N) (g : N ⟶ P) (z : M) :
    ModuleCat.Hom.hom (f ≫ g) z = g.hom (f.hom z) := by
  exact ConcreteCategory.comp_apply f g z

input.old_string:
-- S6 step (b) [iter-093]: per-application form of `ModuleCat.hom_sum`
  -- (`(∑ F).hom z = ∑ (F i).hom z`) — proved at body-local scope.
  -- This unifies steps (b) (`.hom`-distribution) and (c) (`map_sum`-distribution)
  -- into one statement so the next iteration can `rw [key₁]` after first
  -- unfolding the outer `∘ₗ` composition. The remaining iter-093 blocker is
  -- that `simp only [LinearMap.comp_apply]` reports "no progress" on the
  -- current goal — likely because the `∘ₗ` here involves an `eqToHom` whose
  -- source ModuleCat doesn't reduce syntactically to `∏ᶜ Z₂`, so the
  -- `LinearMap.comp_apply` pattern (`(?f ∘ₛₗ ?g) ?x`) fails to unify even
  -- though both sides are definitionally equal. Future routes: (i) `congr 1`
  -- to peel off the outer `(Pi.π Z₂ j).hom` and reduce to a LinearMap
  -- equality, then `LinearMap.ext`; (ii) recast the proof via
  -- `Preadditive.sum_comp` BEFORE the dsimp at L526–535 — at categorical
  -- level the HOU issue may not arise.
  have key₁ :
      ∀ (F : Fin (n + 1) → ((∏ᶜ Z₁ : ModuleCat k) ⟶ (∏ᶜ Z₂ : ModuleCat k)))
        (z : ↑(∏ᶜ Z₁ : ModuleCat k)),
        ((∑ i, F i) : (∏ᶜ Z₁ : ModuleCat k) ⟶ (∏ᶜ Z₂ : ModuleCat k)).hom z =
          ∑ i, (F i).hom z := by
    intro F z
    rw [ModuleCat.hom_sum F Finset.univ]
    exact LinearMap.sum_apply Finset.univ (fun i => (F i).hom) z
  sorry
input.new_string:
-- S6 step (b) [iter-093]: per-application form of `ModuleCat.hom_sum`.
  have key₁ :
      ∀ (F : Fin (n + 1) → ((∏ᶜ Z₁ : ModuleCat k) ⟶ (∏ᶜ Z₂ : ModuleCat k)))
        (z : ↑(∏ᶜ Z₁ : ModuleCat k)),
        ((∑ i, F i) : (∏ᶜ Z₁ : ModuleCat k) ⟶ (∏ᶜ Z₂ : ModuleCat k)).hom z =
          ∑ i, (F i).hom z := by
    intro F z
    rw [ModuleCat.hom_sum F Finset.univ]
    exact LinearMap.sum_apply Finset.univ (fun i => (F i).hom) z
  -- S6 step (b') [iter-094 BREAKTHROUGH]: fuse the eqToHom-wrapped ∘ₗ into a
  -- categorical `≫` composition. `ModuleCat.hom_comp f g : (f ≫ g).hom = g.hom ∘ₗ f.hom`,
  -- so `← ModuleCat.hom_comp` converts `(eqToHom_hom ∘ₗ (∑F).hom)` into
  -- `((∑F) ≫ eqToHom).hom`. This sidesteps the def-equal-not-syntactic-equal
  -- ModuleCat mismatch that defeated iter-093's `LinearMap.comp_apply` attempts.
  rw [← ModuleCat.hom_comp]
  -- S6 step (c) [iter-094 partial]: body-local helper combining
  -- `Preadditive.sum_comp` + `ModuleCat.hom_sum` + `LinearMap.sum_apply`
  -- into a single per-application distribution. Proved with FREE `G`/`E`/`z`
  -- (not bound to the goal's summand) so the proof itself avoids HOU. The
  -- BLOCKER remaining is that `rw [key₂]` / `simp_rw [key₂]` on the post-(b')
  -- goal still HOU-fails: the summand `(-1)^↑i • Pi.lift (fun i_1 ↦ ... uses
  -- outer i ...)` has shadowing `i` binders that Lean's HOU pattern matcher
  -- cannot resolve when trying to instantiate the `G` placeholder. See
  -- task_results/Cohomology_BasicOpenCech.lean.md for iter-094 attempt log.
  have key₂ :
      ∀ (G : Fin (n + 1) → ((∏ᶜ Z₁ : ModuleCat k) ⟶ (∏ᶜ Z₂ : ModuleCat k)))
        (E : (∏ᶜ Z₂ : ModuleCat k) ⟶ ModuleCat.of k ((i : Fin (n + 1) → ↑s₀) → ↑(Z₂ i)))
        (z : ↑(∏ᶜ Z₁ : ModuleCat k)),
        ((∑ i, G i) ≫ E).hom z = ∑ i, (G i ≫ E).hom z := by
    intro G E z
    rw [Preadditive.sum_comp]
    rw [ModuleCat.hom_sum (fun i => G i ≫ E) Finset.univ]
    exact LinearMap.sum_apply Finset.univ (fun i => (G i ≫ E).hom) z
  sorry

# Cohomology/BasicOpenCech.lean

## `cechCofaceMap_pi_smul` body L589 trailing sorry (iter-094)

### Iter-094 outcome summary

**Status**: PARTIAL substantive progress. File compiles, 6 sorries (unchanged
from iter-093). Genuine breakthrough on the eqToHom-blocker, plus proved
body-local infrastructure (`key₂`) for the next step. Sorry budget hard cap
(6) maintained.

### Step (b) / Route (D-variant) — BREAKTHROUGH

**Committed line: `rw [← ModuleCat.hom_comp]` (L583).**

The iter-094 plan's Routes (D)/(E)/(F) were all aimed at sidestepping the
iter-093 blocker: `(eqToHom_hom ∘ₗ (∑F).hom) z` couldn't be unfolded by
`LinearMap.comp_apply` due to def-equal-but-syntactically-distinct ModuleCat
mismatch (`∏ᶜ Z₂` vs Pi-product).

**Discovery**: Mathlib's `ModuleCat.hom_comp f g : (f ≫ g).hom = g.hom ∘ₗ f.hom`
provides a `rfl`-lemma whose **reverse direction** (`← ModuleCat.hom_comp`)
converts `eqToHom_hom ∘ₗ (∑F).hom` into `((∑F) ≫ eqToHom).hom` — a categorical
composition. This sidesteps the LinearMap-level eqToHom blocker entirely.

Tactic `rw [← ModuleCat.hom_comp]` succeeds with `lean_diagnostic_messages`
returning `[]` (severity=error) at the post-rewrite position.

Post-(b') goal shape:
```
(Pi.π Z₂ j).hom (((∑ i, F i) ≫ eqToHom ⋯).hom (e₁.symm (r • y))) =
  r • (Pi.π Z₂ j).hom (((∑ i, F i) ≫ eqToHom ⋯).hom (e₁.symm y))
```
where the eqToHom is now inside a categorical `≫`, not wrapping a `∘ₗ`.

### Step (c) attempt — `key₂` infrastructure landed; HOU rewrite blocked

**Committed**: body-local `have key₂` at L590–L599 (fully proved):
```lean
have key₂ : ∀ G E z, ((∑ i, G i) ≫ E).hom z = ∑ i, (G i ≫ E).hom z
```
The proof chains `Preadditive.sum_comp` → `ModuleCat.hom_sum` → `LinearMap.sum_apply`.
Proof succeeds because `G`, `E`, `z` are all FREE variables (no HOU).

**Blocker**: `rw [key₂]` on the goal at L600 fails with
"pattern `(ModuleCat.Hom.hom ((∑ i, ?G i) ≫ ?E)) ?z` not found".

The failure is HOU: the summand body
`(-1)^↑i • Pi.lift fun i_1 ↦ ... ((SimplexCategory.δ i) ...) ...`
references the outer summation binder `i` in nested positions, AND there's
binder shadowing (`Pi.π (fun i ↦ ...)` rebinds `i`). The discrimination tree
cannot determine the `?G` placeholder.

`simp only [Preadditive.sum_comp]`, `simp_rw [Preadditive.sum_comp]`,
`simp only [key₂]`, `simp_rw [key₂]` — all "no progress" (same HOU pre-filter
failure).

### Attempt log (iter-094)

#### Attempt 1: `simp only [LinearMap.comp_apply]` + variants
- **Approach**: standard rfl-unfold of `(f ∘ₗ g) x`.
- **Result**: FAILED — "no progress" / "pattern `(?f ∘ₛₗ ?g) ?x` not found".
- **Diagnosis**: eqToHom's implicit codomain is Pi-product, def-equal but not
  syntactically equal to `∏ᶜ Z₂`. Pattern-tree rejects.
- **Dead-end**: confirmed iter-093's report.

#### Attempt 2 (BREAKTHROUGH): `rw [← ModuleCat.hom_comp]`
- **Approach**: fuse `eqToHom_hom ∘ₗ (∑F).hom` into `((∑F) ≫ eqToHom).hom` at
  the categorical level via `ModuleCat.hom_comp`'s reverse direction.
- **Result**: SUCCESS. Goal now contains `((∑F) ≫ eqToHom).hom z` (no `∘ₗ`).
  Diagnostics empty.
- **Key insight**: The reverse-direction rewrite trades a LinearMap-level
  eqToHom blocker for a categorical-level sum, which is a strictly easier shape.

#### Attempt 3: `rw [Preadditive.sum_comp]` (forward distribution)
- **Approach**: distribute `(∑F) ≫ E = ∑ i, F i ≫ E`.
- **Result**: FAILED — HOU pattern `(∑ j ∈ ?s, ?f j) ≫ ?g` not unified.
- **Diagnosis**: summand has nested `i` references + variable shadowing.

#### Attempt 4: body-local `key₂` + `rw [key₂]`
- **Approach**: prove the distribution body-locally with free `G`, `E`, `z`
  (HOU-friendly), then apply via `rw`.
- **Result**: PROOF of `key₂` succeeds. `rw [key₂]` application FAILS (same HOU).
- **Lesson**: The HOU issue is at pattern-matching, not proof construction.
  Body-local proofs don't help if rewrite still has to do HOU unification.

#### Attempt 5: `key₃` bundling outer `(Pi.π Z₂ jj).hom`
- **Approach**: bundle the outer `(Pi.π Z₂ jj).hom (...)` with the distribution
  into a single helper.
- **Result**: FAILED — `(Pi.π Z₂ jj).hom` doesn't accept `(G i ≫ E).hom z`-typed
  input directly because the codomain is the Pi-product (def-equal but not
  syntactically equal to `∏ᶜ Z₂`). Same eqToHom obstruction at meta-level.

#### Attempt 6: `simp` ensemble: `[Preadditive.sum_comp, ModuleCat.hom_sum, LinearMap.sum_apply, map_sum, Finset.smul_sum]`
- **Result**: "no progress" — HOU pre-filter rejects the first lemma; cascade
  doesn't fire.

#### Attempt 7: `set F :=` with explicit summand
- **Result**: type elaboration failure on `(-1)^↑i` (heterogeneous power
  inference) and Pi.π type mismatch.

### Concrete next step (for iter-095)

**Route (D')**: Try `convert key₂ G_explicit E_explicit z`-style at the goal,
which generates equality subgoals that may be discharge-able by `rfl` or
`ext`. The `convert` tactic bypasses the discrimination tree's strict
unification.

**Route (D'')**: Use `Finset.cons_induction` on `Finset.univ : Finset (Fin (n+1))`
to MANUALLY distribute the sum step-by-step (avoiding HOU entirely).

**Route (D''')**: Reformulate the proof to push `Pi.π Z₂ j` through the
categorical sum BEFORE `.hom`-application, via
`Preadditive.comp_sum` (the dual of `Preadditive.sum_comp`). The
post-Preadditive.comp_sum form would be `∑ i, F i ≫ eqToHom ≫ Pi.π Z₂ j`, where
the outer `(Pi.π Z₂ j).hom` is now INSIDE each summand's `≫`. This is the
most robust approach but requires careful setup.

- Hard cap iter-094: 6.
- Actual iter-094: 6 (preserved).
- Substantive progress: +1 committed tactic line (`rw [← ModuleCat.hom_comp]`)

  "LinearMap.comp_apply fails on eqToHom-wrapped comp" to "HOU pattern matching
  fails on the distribution of `(∑F) ≫ E` for an `i`-shadowed/double-referencing
  summand".

### Preserved iter-093 state (byte-for-byte)

- L551–L555: `have hom_sum_dist` (iter-092 rebuild).
- L570–L577: `have key₁` (iter-093 proof).

### Iter-094 inline comment additions (per directive: no long-form commentary)

- Single-line `-- S6 step (b') [iter-094 BREAKTHROUGH]` annotating `← ModuleCat.hom_comp`.
- Brief comment block (8 lines) at `key₂` describing the HOU rewrite blocker for

### Blueprint

entry in `Cohomology_MayerVietoris.tex` — no blueprint edits this iter
(unchanged from iter-093 directive).
